content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

November 10, 1976


Leonard Panish, Registrar-Recorder
County of Los Angeles
808 North Spring
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Dear Mr. Panish:

     Thank you for your letter of October 25, 1976, requesting assistance in implementing recent amendments to the Political Reform Act. This letter responds to each of your questions in order.

1.  The staff is preparing an abbreviated committee campaign form for use in the March election, as well as explanatory information concerning Sections 84204.1 and 84204.2. These materials will be sent to you within the next few weeks.

2.  The staff does not intend to issue a revised campaign manual prior to the March election. Instead, as you suggest, we will issue interim information on sheets that are suitable for addition to the existing manual.

3.  If candidates have no reportable transactions whatsoever, the discussion of "period covered" seems somewhat academic because those candidates will not have any information to disclose on their statements. However, for purposes of listing the period covered on the Candidate's Campaign Statement Short Form, Form 470, the candidate should list the date on which he filed the final document necessary to have his name listed on the ballot or write-in votes counted on his behalf as the date from which his report commences.

4.  Pursuant to Section 84207.5 if the appointee to elective office files a post election statement because he received contributions or made expenditures, or gave his consent for any other person to race a contribution or make an expenditure, with a view to bringing about his nomination or election, he is not

Leonard Panish
Page Two
November 10, 1976


required to file a semi-annual campaign statement.
This is because he will have filed a campaign state-
ment in connection with an election held during that
six month period. If the appointee did not engage
in any campaign activity and thus was not required to
file any campaign statements, he must file a semi-
annual statement because he is an elected officer who
has not filed statements in connection with an elec-
tion during the six month period. Section 84206.
However, elected officers whose salaries are less
than one hundred dollars ($100) a month are not re-
quired to file semi-annual statements.

The staff is developing information and guidelines for
the implementation of Sections 82018 and 84204.1 which will be
sent to you in the near future. Although implementation of the
amendments to these sections may present some difficulties, the
staff is of the view that the amendments will simplify report-
ing obligations in the majority of cases while serving the pub-
lic's interest in meaningful disclosure.

If you have any additional questions regarding these
or otherprovisions, please do not hesitate to contact the staff.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

NEW:jo

COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
DEPARTMENT OF REGISTRAR—RECORDER
808 NORTH SPRING, LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA, 90012

LEONARD PANISH
REGISTRAR-RECORDER

CHARLES L. CAMPBELL JR.
CHIEF DEPUTY

October 25, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

Gentlemen and Mesdames:

This is a request for advice and assistance in implementing the provisions
of Chapter 1106, 1976 California Statutes (SB 1362). The request is urgent
because Candidates for the March 8, 1976 Governing Board Elections will
begin filing nomination papers on December 9. Our procedure is to give
each candidate a campaign reporting kit at the time he files his nomination
papers. We anticipate upwards of 500 Candidates.

Our specific concerns are as follows:

1. We will need a reproducible copy of the abbreviated committee
form mandated by Section 84204, 1 (b) no later than November 15.

2. We presently have a stock on hand of approximately 2,000 infor-
mation manuals for campaign disclosure. I assume a revised
manual will be released shortly. It would be most helpful, also,
if the revisions could be designed for insertion in or additional to
the present manual to avoid destroying the existing inventory.

3. Section 83046, which defines "period covered" states that for
persons who have not previously filed a statement the period
covered begins on the day on which the first reportable transaction
occurred. For numerous governing board candidates there will
be no reportable transactions whatsoever. What then is the covering
period? (Our preference is to use the first date on which nomi-
nation papers can be filed for the particular election.)

4. Section 84207, 5 exempts certain appointees to elective office from
filing campaign statements. Does this exemption also apply to the
semi-annual "off year" statement for these same persons if there
have been neither contributions nor expenditures?

Fair Political Practices Commission          -2-          October 25, 1976


We are seriously concerned about two other provisions of these amendments.
First, Sections 82018 and 84204.1 state that "The most recent unabrieviated selection
statement does not refer to any abbreviated statement..." and "the information contained in the abbreviated statement shall be repeated in any subsequent
statement, except...." These changes will necessitate major reprogramming
of our computerized campaign reporting system and a revision of our manual
control systems. Additionally they will have a material impact on our costs
of reviewing campaign statements.

If you can let us know whether or not your commission intends to publish regulations regarding either of these sections soon, we will hold in abeyance
our plans for changing our systems until you have done so.

We are also concerned about the need for a simplified explanation of Section
84204.2. This would be very helpful in the interest of uniform administration
of the act throughout the State; especially if the various jurisdictions provide
it to potential filers.

Thank you for your consideration.

                                        Yours very truly,

                                        [signature]
                                        LEONARD PANISH
                                        Registrar-Recorder


sr

sections.question: 1. We will need a reproducible copy of the abbreviated committee form mandated by Section 84204.1 (b) no later than November 15. 2. We presently have a stock on hand of approximately 2,000 information manuals for campaign disclosure. I assume a revised manual will be released shortly. It would be most helpful, also, if the revisions could be designed for insertion in or addition to the present manual to avoid destroying the existing inventory. 3. Section 83046, which defines "period covered" states that for persons who have not previously filed a statement the period covered begins on the day on which the first reportable transaction occurred. For numerous governing board candidates there will be no reportable transactions whatsoever. What then is the covering period? (Our preference is to use the first date on which nomination papers can be filed for the particular election.) 4. Section 84207.5 exempts certain appointees to elective office from filing campaign statements. Does this exemption also apply to the semi-annual off year statement for these same persons if there have been neither contributions nor expenditures?
sections.conclusion: 1. The staff is preparing an abbreviated committee campaign form for use in the March election, as well as explanatory information concerning Sections 84204.1 and 84204.2. These materials will be sent to you within the next few weeks. 2. The staff does not intend to issue a revised campaign manual prior to the March election. Instead, as you suggest, we will issue interim information on sheets that are suitable for addition to the existing manual. 3. If candidates have no reportable transactions whatsoever, the discussion of "period covered" seems somewhat academic because those candidates will not have any information to disclose on their statements. However, for purposes of listing the period covered on the Candidate's Campaign Statement Short Form, Form 470, the candidate should list the date on which he filed the final document necessary to have his name listed on the ballot or write-in votes counted on his behalf as the date from which his report commences. 4. Pursuant to Section 84207.5, if the appointee to elective office files a post election statement because he received contributions or made expenditures, or gave his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure, with a view to bringing about his nomination or election, he is not required to file a semi-annual campaign statement. If the appointee did not engage in any campaign activity and thus was not required to file any campaign statements, he must file a semi-annual statement because he is an elected officer who has not filed statements in connection with an election during the six month period.
sections.question_synthetic: The Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder seeks FPPC guidance on implementing recent Political Reform Act amendments (SB 1362), including obtaining an abbreviated committee campaign form, revising campaign disclosure manuals, clarifying the "period covered" definition for candidates with no reportable transactions, and determining whether appointees to elective office are exempt from semi-annual filing requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC staff will provide the abbreviated committee form and interim guidance sheets for manual insertion prior to March elections. For candidates with no reportable transactions, the reporting period should commence from the date of filing nomination papers. Appointees to elective office are exempt from semi-annual filing only if they filed post-election statements; otherwise they must file semi-annual statements if they engaged in no campaign activity.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (dated October 25, 1976) and FPPC response (dated November 10, 1976). Extracted primary FPPC response dated November 10, 1976. Response is structured as numbered answers to four specific questions rather than traditional Q/C sections. Minor OCR issues in header text ('tate of Califorll,ia', 'ommissioll') but content is clear. Confident extraction of all substantive conclusions.

QUESTION: 1. We will need a reproducible copy of the abbreviated committee form mandated by Section 84204.1 (b) no later than November 15. 2. We presently have a stock on hand of approximately 2,000 information manuals for campaign disclosure. I assume a revised manual will be released shortly. It would be most helpful, also, if the revisions could be designed for insertion in or addition to the present manual to avoid destroying the existing inventory. 3. Section 83046, which defines "period covered" states that for persons who have not previously filed a statement the period covered begins on the day on which the first reportable transaction occurred. For numerous governing board candidates there will be no reportable transactions whatsoever. What then is the covering period? (Our preference is to use the first date on which nomination papers can be filed for the particular election.) 4. Section 84207.5 exempts certain appointees to elective office from filing campaign statements. Does this exemption also apply to the semi-annual off year statement for these same persons if there have been neither contributions nor expenditures?

CONCLUSION: 1. The staff is preparing an abbreviated committee campaign form for use in the March election, as well as explanatory information concerning Sections 84204.1 and 84204.2. These materials will be sent to you within the next few weeks. 2. The staff does not intend to issue a revised campaign manual prior to the March election. Instead, as you suggest, we will issue interim information on sheets that are suitable for addition to the existing manual. 3. If candidates have no reportable transactions whatsoever, the discussion of "period covered" seems somewhat academic because those candidates will not have any information to disclose on their statements. However, for purposes of listing the period covered on the Candidate's Campaign Statement Short Form, Form 470, the candidate should list the date on which he filed the final document necessary to have his name listed on the ballot or write-in votes counted on his behalf as the date from which his report commences. 4. Pursuant to Section 84207.5, if the appointee to elective office files a post election statement because he received contributions or made expenditures, or gave his consent for any other person to receive a contribution or make an expenditure, with a view to bringing about his nomination or election, he is not required to file a semi-annual campaign statement. If the appointee did not engage in any campaign activity and thus was not required to file any campaign statements, he must file a semi-annual statement because he is an elected officer who has not filed statements in connection with an election during the six month period.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder's urgent requests for guidance on implementing SB 1362 amendments to the Political Reform Act, addressing the need for abbreviated committee forms, campaign disclosure manual revisions, clarification on period-covered definitions, and appointee filing exemptions. Staff commits to providing the abbreviated form and interim information sheets within weeks and clarifies filing requirements for candidates and appointed officials.
